RaceName: Happy Retirement Martin Stoneman Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: HM The King & HM The Queen
Spotlight: Bettered her debut effort when runner-up to subsequent Listed-placed Sacred Ground at Yarmouth (1m, good) last October but she was disappointing on her reappearance at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) 27 days ago, dropping away after racing too freely; that may have taken the freshness out of her but she needs to settle better on her first try over 1m2f.

RaceName: Happy Retirement Martin Stoneman Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dam is Powerful Breeze who won the May Hill at two and went close in the Fillies' Mile; 200-1 on her debut at Newbury (1m1f, good to soft) two weeks ago but she showed promise, finishing fifth of 11; open to progress.

RaceName: Happy Retirement Martin Stoneman Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Promising debut when a 5l second to subsequent Ribblesdale winner Earth Shot at Newmarket (1m2f, good) but didn't see the trip out when turned over at a short price over 1m3f at Kempton (AW) 15 days ago; could yet build on her debut encouragement.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 7,000gns yearling; second foal; brother to 6f AW winner Your Love (RPR 88); dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/AW; 93); in-form stable sent out a 2yo to make a winning debut this month; check betting.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 10-1 when slowly away and never in contention in a novice at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 24 days ago; open to improvement but it is essential.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: $105,000 yearling; first foal of a US 5f dirt winner, herself a half-sister to two winners; Irish trainer had a well-backed newcomer win at Goodwood on debut this month; betting instructive.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling, 75,000gns breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Seacruiser (1m 2yo; RPR 104) and Velerin (Spanish 7.5f-1m4f); dam 6f-1m2f maiden (67), half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winners Across The Stars and Bronze Cannon; enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Smart pedigree and he was sent off the 2-1 favourite for his debut at Sandown (5f, good), eventually coming home in third having made a big move in the middle part of the race; stepping up to 6f will suit and Charlie Appleby won this race with a 2yo having his second run 12 months ago.

RaceName: It's Racing Staff Week EBF 'Confined' Novice Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Supremacy half-brother to useful mudlark Room Service; shaped well when keeping on for third at Salisbury (6f, good) 11 days ago; just about sets the standard and with improvement seeming highly likely.
Gained his fourth success when making all at Bath (1m6f, good to firm) last month and ran another respectable race when a 2l fourth of seven over this C&D 25 days ago; contender.

13-race maiden but she's been placed in a handicap at Newcastle (2m, AW) and a classified event at Windsor (11.5f, good to soft) on her last two outings; steps back up in trip with claims if handling this quicker ground.

1-20, with the win coming in a classified event at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW; first-time blinkers) last February; runner-up on three occasions, including on good to firm at Chepstow last summer, but was well held at Yarmouth (soft) 15 days ago.
24-race maiden but he has posted some solid efforts, including when third of 14 at Catterick (1m4f, good) last month; now tries 1m6f for the first time.

Well backed when winning a Navan fillies' maiden on her debut last September (1m, good); flopped on heavy going on her only other start last year and sold out of Dermot Weld's stable for 190,000euros in February; likely there is more in the tank and stable won this race 12 months ago (with a 3yo).

Kept on well to make a winning debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November; that bare form is modest and she displayed improvement when third of seven in a 1m fillies' novice at Goodwood three weeks ago; further progress required to defy her penalty but it isn't out of the question back down in trip.
Consistent in defeat as a 2yo (7f-1m) and possible soft ground contributed to her weak finish at Salisbury 16 days ago; could resume her progress back up on a faster surface.
Stable going well but she needs to leave the form of her first two runs well behind her if she is to win.

Seventh foal; closely related to 6f/7f winner Cold Front (including Saudi Arabia; RPR 106), half-sister to three winners including Brewing (6f/7f AW; 106) and Showering (6f; 95); dam 7f winner (83), half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Enticing; blinkered for her debut; market useful.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Six-time winner (6f/7f), including when coming out on top in a match race under Joe Bradnam at Yarmouth (7f, soft) two weeks ago; has form on the AW over 1m but a 5lb rise for that latest success demands a bit more.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Gained his fifth success when pulling 4l clear at Lingfield (7f, good) last month and he's finished third in both starts since, including over 1m at Kempton 15 days ago; each-way chance if seeing out this slightly longer trip.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Won three times last year, the most recent coming at Ripon (1m2f, good) off a 4lb higher mark than today's; went close at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) in April but he's been below his best in two starts since; tongue-tie is added to cheekpieces.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Last three wins have been on the AW, the most recent off a 6lb higher mark at Newcastle (7f) in December; below his best there over 1m after becoming upset in the stalls 90 days ago and needs to bounce back returned to turf on his first start since.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Both wins on turf have been over 7.5f at Lingfield on good to firm; latest win came over 1m on AW at Kempton in January off 3lb lower; below par under a penalty in first-time cheekpieces (retained) 141 days ago and has been off since.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Sole win from 21 starts came at Bath (1m, good) in April; bounced back from a poor effort when runner-up over this C&D last month and shaped as if still in good form at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago, leaving the impression the return to 1m would suit; chance.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Dual winner, most recently at Hamilton (8.5f, good) last month when ridden by Jake Dickson; wasn't at his best there on good to soft 15 days ago but could bounce back; makes the shortlist.

RaceName: Cambion Electronics 65 Years In Manufacturing "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excel')
Spotlight: Came from off the pace when winning at Southwell (7f, AW) in March but he's been below that level in three starts since; all five wins on turf have been at Brighton and others have stronger claims.
On a losing run but he retains plenty of ability and his latest fifth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) was another solid effort; should make another bold bid.

OwnerName: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields
Spotlight: Finished second in the corresponding race 12 months ago when rated 4lb higher; back to winning ways at Pontefract (6f, good) this month and his latest C&D third was another good run; place claims once again.

Off the mark at the third attempt as a 2yo, making most at Lingfield (6f, AW) in December; gelded soon after; makes his handicap debut at a realistic level and he is one to take seriously back from a 192-day break.
7f Catterick maiden win as a 2yo; two solid runs this year sandwich a modest effort on the Rowley course; Ryan Moore returns to the saddle; still has low mileage and he can't be ruled out.

Beaten in three handicaps at a lower level this year, including when the warm favourite for his seasonal return (1m, good to firm); sprinting looks worth exploring but he will need a clear personal best to take this.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 10,000euros yearling; colt by Cotai Glory; first foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 77), sister to 6f Group 3 winner Happy Romance (including 2yo), out of unraced half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Warsaw; one to note in the betting on his debut.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Caught the eye making some good late progress at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) on his debut last month but was never involved at Salisbury (6f, soft) 16 days ago after colliding with the rail early on; should yet do better.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; colt by Blackbeard; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Samurai Mike (RPR 91); dam Italian 7f/7.5f 2yo winner (including Listed; 98), half-sister to Japanese 1m Listed winner Bellagio Bond, out of Italian 1,000 Guineas winner; interesting newcomer and he's worth a market check.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Dam once-raced half-sister to US stakes-placed 8.5f winner Tarneema; showed ability when third of 13 on his debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm; 80-1) ten days ago and may well come on for that experience; stepping up to 6f should suit.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Last of ten on his debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and again failed to beat a rival home there ten days ago; steps up to 6f with a huge step forward needed.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Well held in his first two starts but showed much more at Chester (6f, good to soft) 13 days ago, finishing second of nine; needs to back that up but has claims if doing so.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: 9,000euros yearling, resold for 4,000gns; colt by Supremacy; ninth foal; half-brother to four winners including Rebel At Dawn (5f including 2yo/Bahrain; RPR 101), Cargin Bhui (5f/6f AW; 86) and Ermin Street (1m AW; 83); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (64), half-sister to Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Umneyati; nice pedigree and the betting should guide.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Fifth at Ripon (6f, good) on her second outing before finishing runner-up at Redcar (6f, good to firm) and Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) on her next two starts; below her best over 5f at Hamilton (good to soft) eight days ago but could bounce back; solid place claims.

She did not have to improve on her first two runs to capitalise on a golden opportunity at Ripon (6f, good) last week; in deeper waters today and her in-form trainer may hold stronger claims with Havana Sprite.

Well-bred filly; shaped with promise amidst inexperience on her debut at Nottingham (6f, good) three weeks ago; can do better but this is a tough assignment and Hugo Palmer holds more obvious claims with Glorious Game.
Made all for a comfortable success at Windsor (6f, good to soft; tongue tied) on her debut 17 days ago; Oisin Murphy retains the ride and she is open to further progress; one to consider.

Promising debut at Newmarket last month and she bumped into a well-regarded opponent at Yarmouth on her second start; made no mistake in a small field at Bath (5f, good) 12 days ago; likely to appreciate the extra furlong and she is high on the shortlist.

Scrambled home at Pontefract (5f, good) on her second start but she was 33-1 for a York Listed event last month and was always in rear; tongue-tie and cheekpieces now tried; other have stronger claims.
18-1 for her debut at Windsor (6f, good to soft) 17 days ago, a race where she kept on for second behind Glorious Game; work to do to turn the tables with that rival but she is open to improvement.

Improved for the step up to 6f when winning a fillies' novice at Ripon (good ground) last month; not seen to best effect in the Hilary Needler at Beverley last time; could have a bigger effort in her back at 6f.
Promise in three runs in Ireland; well-beaten 66-1 shot on her first run for this yard but that was in the Group 3 Albany Stakes at Royal Ascot last week; major player in calmer waters.

RaceName: Neigh More Early Starts For Martin Stoneman Handicap
Spotlight: His nine wins have been on good ground or slower, with six of them coming at Chester; caught wide there on his reappearance and was well held in seventh there over 7f 13 days ago; drops back in trip with cheekpieces back on.

RaceName: Neigh More Early Starts For Martin Stoneman Handicap
Spotlight: 1-9, with the success coming in a novice at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last June; has posted some good efforts since, including when narrowly beaten at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) in August, but she's been below her best in two starts this year; hung left last time and cheekpieces are now tried.

RaceName: Neigh More Early Starts For Martin Stoneman Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 17th attempt when winning at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last month and followed up when ridden by Chrstian Howarth over that same C&D 12 days ago, making all; he did it well last time and 9lb rise may not stop a bold bid for the hat-trick.

RaceName: Neigh More Early Starts For Martin Stoneman Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly came out on top when winning at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in October on his second start, but he hung left when failing to beat a rival home on his handicap debut at Kempton (6f, AW; first-time tongue-tie) 78 days ago; tongue-tie is discarded for his turf debut.

RaceName: Neigh More Early Starts For Martin Stoneman Handicap
Spotlight: Progressed with each of his three starts in novice/maiden company (7f/1m) but he didn't see things out on his handicap debut at Chester (7.5f, soft; first start since being gelded) 13 days ago; pedigree doesn't suggest this drop to 6f is what he wants.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: High-class handicapper at 7f/1m; winless since landing a valuable 7f handicap at Ascot in July 2024 but he is now 6lb lower and three runs in Bahrain this winter suggested he retained a chunk of ability; back from four months off and this is perhaps not the day to catch him.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: The winner of three of his seven starts; sent off the red-hot favourite for Ascot's Victoria Cup last month but he failed to fire, finishing 22nd of the 28 runners; gelded soon after and he remains capable of better.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: On a handy mark on last summer's peak efforts for Hugo Palmer; ran some fair races in Dubai for new connections earlier in the year; others appeal a bit more for win purposes.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Not straightforward but he is a capable performer on his day and he landed his first turf success when beating Silver Ghost on the Rowley course six weeks ago, again hanging right under pressure but full value for the neck winning margin; up 3lb but has the ability to feature.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Promising stable debut in the race won by Superposition last month but he hasn't built on it in either start since; due to be 2lb lower from the weekend; others look stronger.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Finished third in the 2022 Dewhurst; getting on for three years since his last win but he ran well for second at Sandown (1m, good) on his penultimate start; front-running fifth of 15 at Newbury two week ago; new cheekpieces need to spark something extra.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: The addition of cheekpieces saw him break his turf duck at Yarmouth in April, digging deep to win by a short head; similar form when third of 14 at Goodwood (7f, good) five weeks ago; he will need a personal best to come out on top this time though.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Infrequent winner but he is well handicapped, has strong C&D form and his latest York run is easy to strike a line through; Ryan Moore retains the ride; dangerous if getting the breaks.

RaceName: Hallgarten Partner Of The Jockey Club Handicap
OwnerName: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner
Spotlight: Not at his best two weeks ago but he had been threatening on both AW and turf beforehand; on a dangerous mark and he can't be discounted.
Dual winner over 5f, the most recent coming at Redcar in April on good to firm; below his best in two starts since but has an each-way chance.

Gained his fifth success when winning at Catterick (5f, good) last month off a 2lb lower mark than today's and he was narrowly denied at Thirsk (5f, good) nine days ago, bouncing back from a lesser effort; contender.

OwnerName: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: All three wins have been on the AW at Wolverhampton over 5f but he caught the eye finishing strongly for fourth at Thirsk (5f, good; Irish Dancer was second) nine days ago; possibly an unfortunate loser on that occasion having been hampered against the rail and and could make amends.

Has gained all eight of her wins over further (6f-1m); finished off well to be beaten around 2l over 5f on the AW at Lingfield 97 days ago; returns to turf having been off since and will need a good pace to aim at.

Sole win from 21 starts came in a classified event at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last August; finished third at Catterick and Doncaster in April but she's been below that level on her last three starts.

Won two of her six starts last season, a 1m maiden at Windsor (good to firm) and a 1m handicap on the Rowley course at Newmarket (good); good second in a Class 3 fillies' handicap when last seen in October; has more to offer this year but this race hasn't been won by an older filly since 2016.
She has run some fair races for this yard without suggesting she was ahead of her mark; suspicion she will prove vulnerable once again.

Improved on her 2yo form when winning an 8.6f handicap at Wolverhampton last month; further progress when second, beaten a head, to an improver at Windsor 12 days later; up another 4lb today but she's on the right path.

Confirmed the promise of her debut by winning a fillies' novice at Bath (1m, firm) last month; no threat in a Class 4 handicap at Sandown four weeks ago but this shouldn't be so demanding; still has potential.

Runner-up on her first two starts (6f, AW); got up late to win a 6f fillies' maiden at Leicester last month but that form has taken some knocks since; could progress for the extra 2f but she needs to if she is to overcome this mark.

OwnerName: Hold My Beer, Gee Gees Racing & N Clark
Spotlight: More exposed than the other 3yos but she arrives on the back of two placed efforts (behind Crystal Pier on the first occasion); should run her race but perhaps vulnerable to improvers.

Some late headway when fifth at Doncaster (6f; 16-1) on penultimate start, and a similar story over the sharp 7f at Musselburgh subsequently; mark continues to ease and best run for previous trainer was on AW at Lingfield; no forlorn hope.

OwnerName: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster
Spotlight: C&D winner, who was beaten by a short-head on only other visit (6f) to Newcastle; won off this mark at Wolverhampton (7f) last October, but his last two efforts were low key - last of nine at Goodwood latest; first-time headgear needs to have a reviving effect.

OwnerName: A Scaife, C Scaife, G Scaife
Spotlight: Ex-Clive Cox; touched off at Kempton (7f; 13-2) on final start for that trainer; picked up for 24,000gns soon after, but didn't shine on Ripon stable debut (1m; sixth of 11) ten weeks ago; shorter trip and AW return are helpful.

Upped-in-trip and free when seventh in a 7f Chester fillies' maiden (80-1; beaten over 6l) four weeks ago; that form has been franked (first two subsequent winners) but this 135,000gns yearling will need to settle better on AW/handicap debut; worth a market check.

50-1 winner over 1m here (first start since wind surgery) in March; has failed to beat a rival in two subsequent starts, tailed off (beaten 58l) at Doncaster six weeks ago; difficult to know what to expect.

Comfortable winner here (1m, Class 6; 6-4 favourite, from 7lb lower) last November; less productive on Nottingham comeback run; fifth of 14 at Bath (1m, good) 12 days ago was better, and he's noted over 7f at a track which suits; Harry Vigors takes off a handy 5lb.

Won on handicap debut at Redcar (7f, Class 6; 16-1) on penultimate start; that form is okay (second and third have won since, the fourth beaten by a head at Catterick on Monday); however, was awash with sweat and took a fierce grip when flopping at Doncaster next time; risks attached.

With William Haggas when winning in newly applied blinkers here (6f; 15-2; Surgeon Commander third) in January; failed to shine in three runs for Martin Dunne; stumbled badly at the start on first go for this trainer at Doncaster latest; that's excusable, and an easing mark is helpful.

OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Didn't show much on her first two starts (C&D); slightly better when back from a six-month plus break at Thirsk (7f maiden; 25-1, fifth of ten) recently; others more obvious, but this is still a suitable level to make her handicap debut.

Sole win from 20 starts came on AW at Southwell (7f) in March; has plenty of placed efforts on her CV, and didn't get the breaks when sixth (Surgeon Commander fourth) at Doncaster latest; should give her running from what is a reasonably competitive mark.

Made the breakthrough at Wolverhampton (7f, Class 6; 3-1) three weeks ago, the return to that track offered as a possible reason for the improved performance; slightly more needed over this stiff, straight 7f from a 6lb higher mark, but still one of the likeliest candidates.

Beaten a nose by stablemate Surgeon Commander over C&D in January; won off a 4lb lower mark at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) in April, and on the premises twice since, backing out of contention only late on at Carlisle (fourth) ten days ago; task isn't impossible.

Possesses form lines with a number of these, including Maynora, Henriette Ronner, and Popty Ping; 2-2 over C&D (nose win and a dead-heat) and was bang there until the final furlong (fourth) at Doncaster 11 days ago; likely player.

80,000gns yearling; had only just turned two when a promising fourth on his debut over C&D last month; finished third of four at Carlisle (6f, soft) last time but he wasn't beaten far and that was behind the subsequent Albany third Light Of Dawn; should continue to improve with experience and he's respected back on AW.

100,000gns yearling by Havana Grey; made a solid start when third of ten at Ascot (6f, good; 2-1 favourite) last month and he built on that with a creditable fourth in a York novice two weeks later; travelled well for a long way last time and he sets a good standard on that form; key player.

Beaten 7l when third at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) and he failed to build on that when a remote fifth at Beverley (7.4f, good); may have had stamina issues last time but he has plenty to find here and nurseries will be more suitable.
Weak 18-1 shot and he finished tenth of 11 on his Ripon debut (6f, good) last week.
Struggled at 50-1 on debut at Musselburgh (7f, good) this month and he's probably a longer-term prospect.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 350,000gns yearling; finished down the field at York (6f) and Thirsk (7f, both on good) last month but he ran well to a point last time; has quite a lot to find on this switch to AW but it's still early days and he's a possible improver on this drop back in trip.

£58,000 yearling; closely related to four winners including Ragga Bomb (6f/1m including 2yo/Hong Kong; RPR 94), Aqua Bear (7f AW 2yo; 81) and Bare Necessity (6f AW; 74), half-brother to four other winners; dam 6f 2yo winner (89); interesting newcomer and he needs watching in market.

Reappeared with respectable seventh of 21 in the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and built on that when going down by a nose at Hamilton (extended 1m, good) three weeks ago; while his improvement since being gelded has come at around 1m his fourth over 1m2f at Nottingham last June was as good as anything he'd produced at that time; much respected on AW debut.

Four French wins for Andre Fabre and thereabouts for new trainers in four consecutive races in Dubai this January-March, controlling a steadily run race from the front when 4l winner of 1m4f handicap (good) on final occasion; weakened to finish well held in 1m4f York Dante meeting handicap on British debut but the fact he's finished in the first three on all four occasions he's tackled an artificial surface (including his best RPR) provides hope now back on AW.
Multiple AW winner, the latest over 1m4f at Southwell in November; off since a below-par run at Kempton in January; no great record fresh and possibly best watched on this first visit to Newcastle.

Added to his excellent Kempton record with wins over 1m3f and 1m4f this year; another cracking effort there when chasing home the progressive Gamrai over 1m3f in March; probably worth ignoring a recent run on turf but his peak AW form (including all wins) has been achieved on Polytrack.
Useful winner over 1m2f and 1m3f on turf for James Owen last season; raced freely and likely needed his 1m3f Carlisle return for new yard 26 days ago; promising third on debut on previous AW start.

7yo who is lightly raced for his age and made it 6-12 on AW when winning a competitive C&D handicap over Easter; failed to get heavily involved back on turf at the York Dante meeting since but has to enter calculations now back on AW.

Secured the latest of his three wins when taking Redcar handicap (1m, good) in May; couple of respectable efforts over 1m2f on turf since and he hasn't finished out of the first three in four AW starts (all here); another who can't be dismissed.

Bounced back from a disappointing handicap debut on turf with a win over C&D last October; weakened to finish well held on 1m3f York reappearance 13 days ago but it's possible this lightly raced 4yo simply needed that first outing for eight months (gelded).

Ex-French soft-ground winner; gelded ahead of finishing a solid fourth at Chester (10.3f, good) on his stable debut last month; outpaced over 1m2f on a fast surface at Newbury since; remains early days with this shrewd stable; non-runner 5.05 Carlisle Wednesday.
His 2-3 AW record includes a comfortable C&D win off only 1lb lower last September; arrives on the back of two good efforts in strong turf handicaps and a bold bid looks assured now back on AW.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Soft-ground debut winner who comfortably landed a Doncaster handicap (1m, good) last September; still looks feasibly treated on that form but he's been beaten 11l and 18l in Listed events in his last two starts; has bit to prove after another break and he's untried on AW.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Well treated on his Listed win at Sandown in July 2024 but he drew a blank during his 2025 Australian campaign and has been well held in both runs (1m, good to firm/good) for new yard this season; still unexposed on AW but he needs to raise his game back in this sphere.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Often held up in rear, so he saw very little daylight when 50-1 off career-low mark in the Spring Cup at Newbury in April; dropped another 2lb since but he last won in September 2023 and failed to make an impact in all but one of his races in 2025, so does not look the percentage call.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Newbury handicap (7f, good) last April and he's reached the frame in his last five starts including when fourth of 24 in the Spring Cup (1m, good) on his penultimate run; looks interesting off same mark here and he ran well on his only previous attempt on AW; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have been on AW and he went close in his bid for a double at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in March; never involved back on turf at Ascot in May but he started slowly and was hampered at a crucial stage; now goes back up in grade but he has possibilities back on Tapeta.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Big improver for this yard last year, winning three times (good to firm/AW) including when making all at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in July; remains feasibly treated on that form but he's been out of sorts in both runs this season and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: All seven wins have been on AW and latest was over C&D in January; found more progress when a close third off this mark at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) in April but he's been tailed off back on turf in last two runs; now returns to scene of his last success but he needs to bounce back near best.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins split between turf and AW; his last success was off this mark at Lingfield in December but he has a mixed record since and was a well-held fourth at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) last month; others preferred.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo, who has form figures of 223 this season and that latest third was a bold bid in a Ripon handicap (1m2f, good) last month; still has scope for further progress and he's respected on this drop back in trip.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Made it 2-5 when making all in Yarmouth handicap (1m2f, good to firm) on his reappearance in April; creditable third at Newbury the following month but he flopped when joint-favourite at Redcar last time; has bit to prove again after that and he could have competition for the lead back on AW.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Kippax Seaton Delaval Handicap
Spotlight: Made a winning debut here (7f) early last year and was a good third at Goodwood (1m, good) in his final run last season; left William Haggas for 16,000gns in October; never dangerous on stable debut at Redcar last week but he didn't get the best of runs and was only beaten around 3l; should be sharper this time and he's in the mix back on AW.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 66-1 when a 15l sixth of seven at York (6f, good) on his debut 12 days ago but the dams' side of his pedigree suggests this longer trip will suit; switches to the AW and represents a stable that won a division of this in 2024 and 2025.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Gelding by Alkumait; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Tee Aitch Aye (1m1f; RPR 74) and Believe Me Now (7f AW/1m turf; 57); dam unraced half-sister to winners Khafeef (French/NZ 1m2f-1m3f) and Finn Russell (1m-1m2f), out of 1m winner; best watched on his debut unless the market speaks favourably.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed ability on his debut at Pontefract (6f, good) and ran to a similar level third of 11 at Ripon (6f, good) a week ago; leaves the impression this step up to 7f will suit on his AW debut.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: Sarah Fern, Patricia James And Partner
Spotlight: Didn't show much on his debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) but took a big step forward at Leicester (7f, good) 17 days ago, finishing second of ten; he was keeping on at the line again on that occasion and could be suited by this stiff finish on his AW debut.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Met trouble when down the field at Beverley (5f, good to soft) on his second start and ran respectably in a York novice (6f, good; third was placed in the Norfolk) 33 days ago; each-way claims on his AW debut if seeing out the longer trip.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Half-brother to Qatari 5.5-9.5f winner Due To Dance (including dirt); looked in need of the experience when down the field on his debut at Carlisle (6f, good; 33-1) ten days ago and now steps up in trip; may be one for later on.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Half-sister to French winners Sandon (1m2f 2yo) and Betty Nelson (10.5f); shaped with plenty of promise when third of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft; 6l clear of the rest), leaving the impression stepping up to 7f would suit; good claims if handling the switch to the AW.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed more than she did on her debut when fourth of seven at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) ten days ago; now switches to the AW and she's open to further improvement.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Dam French 7.5f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner (RPR 103); did best of the newcomers when fifth of 11 in a seller at Chester (7f, good to soft; 11-1) on her debut 12 days ago and she may well build on that; yard won a division of this last year.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Filly by Ardad; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Wick Powell (5f-7f including 2yo Listed/AW; RPR 96) and Londonness (Japanese 4.5f-7f dirt); dam unplaced 1m (39), half-sister to prolific 6f-1m1f winner Give It Some Teddy, out of 7f 2yo Listed winner; market should be informative on her debut.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: £7,000 yearling; sixth foal; closely related to French 1m2f AW/10.5f turf winner Caligine (RPR 87); dam Listed-placed 1m winner (100), half-sister to Listed-placed 7f-8.6f winner Quick Change, out of half-sister to 6.5f/7f Group 1 winner Occupandiste; has good pedigree and he needs checking in market on debut.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Beaten around 10l in two 6f novice events (AW/good) and latest was in a small field at Hamilton; still early days but he needs a transformation upped in trip.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Well-held fourth in two 7f maidens (good) but both those runs were behind useful rivals and he has leading form claims in this line-up; respected.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Tailed off on Redcar debut (6f, good) last month before a well-held eighth of ten in similar conditions at Carlisle; now goes up in trip but he has lots to find and nureries will be more suitable.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 15,000euros yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart 1m-1m2f winner Ajalo, out of German 1m3f winner; yard 0-19 with 2yos in recent years and he could be a longer-term prospect.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to five winners including Royal Ashirah (Italian 5f-6f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 94) and Portugueseprincess (6f AW; 87); dam 6f winner (81), half-sister to useful 7f-8.4f winner Colour Blue, out of 5f Group 3 winner; makes debut in an ordinary race and market should guide.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Took a step forward in first-time blinkers when a never-nearer fourth of ten in 6f Carlisle maiden on recent stable debut (formerly with Karl Burke); that form puts her firmly in the picture and she looks interesting on this step up in trip.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
OwnerName: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner
Spotlight: 50-1 and she finished a remote last of eight at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) four weeks ago; stablemate of Mottaret.

RaceName: True Colours Charity Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; second foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to winners Kitodan (US 10.5f Grade 3), El Cap (useful 1m) and Market King (US Graded stakes-placed 1m/8.5f); yard has won a division of this race with newcomers in last two years and she needs a close look on debut.

Ended his 2yo campaign by shading a tight finish 5f Leicester novice (good to soft) in October; returned with a couple of respectable placed efforts over 6f/5f this spring but ran poorly back at Leicester latest; has a visor added on the back of that and also switches to AW for the first time.

Ready winner of a four-runner 5f fillies' novice at Musselburgh (soft) last autumn (final run for Kevin Philippart de Foy); seems to be gradually getting her act together for her new yard, with latest York fourth (I'm Dan Dare 4l ahead in second) of 13 another slight step back in the right direction; a further 2lb ease in her mark will help her get more competitive.
Won a 6f nursery course nursery in December and also a close second over C&D last month (first run since being gelded); not so good on turf since but better can be expected back on AW.

Promise for Adrian Nicholls and plenty to spare when making all in a 5f handicap at Catterick on stable debut; bettered that form when neck second (Yellow Diamond 4l behind in fourth) of 13 at York (5f, good) 13 days ago, keeping on well despite losing his left cheekpiece approaching the final furlong; raised another 5lb but the third, Schrodinger's Cat, won easily at Redcar on Saturday; should have a big say on AW debut.

Improved when keeping on from off pace for third over 6f at Thirsk last week, which was his first attempt at a sprint trip and his second handicap; didn't obviously shape as if crying out for drop to 5f but he brings unexposed potential.

Ended 2yo season with 5f Lingfield novice win (Polytrack) but well-held seventh this term in 6f handicaps at Ripon (reared start) and this venue; first-time blinkers and return to 5f need to spark a revival.
Consistent rather than progressive as a 2yo and has failed to make a significant impact in handicaps this year, fading into a well-held sixth behind I'm Dan Dare and Yellow Diamonds at York latest.

Has shown promise on several occasions, including over 6f here; matched her best form on handicap debut at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) last month and creditable fourth in a novice at Wetherby (5.5f, good) since; enters calculations.

Scored off 3lb higher over 5f at Wolverhampton in February for Martin Dunne; better on turf for new yard when respectable 3.5l third of six at Leicester (5f) 17 days ago; needs recapture her best now back on AW.

Headgear and a switch to minor handicaps have seen good progress since November, including scoring twice over 6f here; won again at Catterick (6f, good; made all) last month and good third of 14 at Doncaster (6f again) since; while best form has been at 6f, the speed he's shown in recent starts suggests he should be equally effective at 5f; likely to go well.

Improved when justifying support with a brave win on nursery debut at Redcar (5f, good; 2-1) last autumn; hasn't reproduced that form in two outings this year and perhaps best watched unless the betting hints at a revival.
Two C&D handicap wins this year, latter off 1lb lower than today; found it tougher on turf since but could bounce back returned to AW having been gelded.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner, the latest in May 2024, and he ran okay in February on his last hurdle start; he may have needed the run when back on the Flat three weeks ago and is not ruled out off a reduced mark.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: He's 2lb lower than when winning at Brighton last August but has struggled to get competitive this year and needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: 17-race maiden who was well beaten on AW last winter on his last two starts; however, he's well handicapped on last season's best efforts, many of which came here, and Eireann Cagney has won the last two runnings of this race; no surprise to see a big performance.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: 8yo who won here (1m3f, good) last Wednesday on his second start back from a break; he'll be fine back up in trip and Kayleigh Williams made a dream start to her riding career when winning on a stablemate this month; strong claims under a 4lb penalty.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 12th attempt when winning here (1m4f, good) this month; inconsistent and was only fifth of seven behind stablemate Annandale here (1m3f, good) last Wednesday but he's not written off.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: Just one win from his 22 starts and beaten 6l when fourth in first-time visor (retained) at Ayr (1m5f, soft) last Friday; however, that trip-and-ground combination may have stretched his stamina and two starts ago he was short-headed by Uncle Liam here (1m4f, good); he's not discounted.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Saints & Sinners Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (The Saints & Sinners Challenge Cup)
Spotlight: Placed a few times this year but well beaten at Newcastle two starts ago and she hung badly left and unseated her rider at Ripon last Thursday; remains a maiden after 20 starts and others make greater appeal.

Justified favouritism at Redcar (1m2f; good to firm) before following up at Doncaster (1m, good to soft); carries 5lb extra for latest win (and now rated above this 0-55 grade); Cian Horgan's claim reduces the weight burden; player if able to run to similar level back on AW (0-11).

Southwell winner (7f); couldn't hold off a late challenge at Doncaster (7f; 13-2) on penultimate start; restless in the stalls and ran too free when favourite at Beverley latest; perhaps a few pounds too high in the weights.

Four C&D wins; attracted market support and looked an unlucky loser, hitting the line strongly after being hampered at a crucial stage over C&D (100-30 favourite, beaten a head) three weeks ago; still fairly weighted after a small upward nudge; holds an obvious chance.
No win since December 2024 (1m4f); weakened from 2f out on recent stable debut at Redcar (1m2f; 14-1, beaten 15l by Woodleigh); possibly needed that first run for 278 days but others are preferred.

C&D winner (2023); not far behind Barleybrown and Abu Royal here this month, but declined a winning opportunity (carried head high and idled) next time; visor didn't work at Beverley last week, so question marks persist.
Veteran (13-103), latest win C&D (from 5lb higher) on New Year's Day; out of sorts lately, so perhaps age is beginning to catch up with him.

OwnerName: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club
Spotlight: 1-30, the win over C&D from a similar mark last September; lost out in a tight finish to a stablemate of Woodleigh at Beverley (1m2f, good; 4-1) last week; no reason why she shouldn't take the swift turnaround in her stride; possible.

1-26, the success over three years ago; drop back in trip (and refitted cheekpieces) coincided with overdue return to form, when a closing third to Dinah Myte (Barleybrown second) over C&D 23 days ago; his odds will be shorter now, but whether he'll back it up is questionable.

Southwell 7f win; close fourth over 7f here last December; flashed home late, having had no luck in running, over C&D (seventh of 13) recently; Robert Havlin booked in this bid to get closer to Barleybrown, Abu Royal and co.

Came from off a strong pace to win over C&D (16-1; from 2lb higher) in February, and maintained a similar level of form (253) for a short while afterwards; less convincing on turf lately, only sixth of 14 at Beverley last week; stablemate of Shimmering Spirit.

0-17; runner-up at Beverley (8.5f; 14-1) last week, a track which seems to bring out the best in her; will probably find a winning opportunity eventually, and was placed in a 0-65 at last year's Plate festival.
Hasn't shown anything significant yet, tailed off on return from a nine-week break at Ripon (1m, good; 25-1) last week; cheekpieces are newly applied; this stablemate of I Can Boogy makes no appeal.

Eight-race maiden; weakened (third) in an 8.5f Class 6 at Nottingham on penultimate start; significant drop off from that level at Wetherby (1m) last week; cheekpieces newly fitted for AW return; better to focus elsewhere.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Well held when fifth of ten on debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 24 days ago but was 15-8 favourite for that and is presumably thought capable of much better; recently gelded.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Well down the field on last month's debut at Newmarket (6f) and lost all chance at the start when dropped to 5f at Windsor a couple of weeks later (gelded soon afterwards); improvement required.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Flopped when favourite for debut but took big step forward and put in good front-running shift when second of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) 16 days ago; big player if suited by this different trip-and-ground combination.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: £5,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; fourth foal; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 95), half-sister to winners Queen Of Deauville (5f 2yo) and Investigation (11.4f); the only newcomer in the line-up.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Out of a useful 5f 2yo winner; 40-1 for debut at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago but displayed promise before fading into sixth; can build on that performance here and goes on the shortlist.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Out of a 5f-winning half-sister to Breeders Cup Dirt Mile winner Furthest Land; unable to justify prominence in the betting when always behind in quite valuable Bath maiden (5.7f, good) three weeks ago but ought to have learnt from that experience.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Failed to build upon fairly encouraging debut (5.7f, firm) when last of five (albeit not beaten far) at Ffos Las (5f, soft) last week but may do so now she's back on fast ground.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Respectable sixth of 12 at Ascot (5f, good) on debut in eary May and perhaps found 6f on soft ground too taxing when below that form at Goodwood a fortnight ago; remains a filly to be interested in.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Did pretty well to finish fourth of ten on debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in May, given she didn't settle in the early stages, but she was below that form after again racing too freely at Lingfield (6f, AW); drops in trip for this turf debut but others look safer.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Soundly beaten fifth of 11 at 11-1 on recent debut at Pontefract (6f, good) but that doesn't tell the full story as she was slowly away and made good headway around the home turn before her run petered out; she's a likely improver and the drop to 5f could be a good move for the time being.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Slowly away and always towards the rear on debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; however, she was sent off at just 11-4 and it will be interesting to see if she's again prominent in the betting.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Promising third at 11-2 on last month's debut at Thirsk (5f, good) and this Newmarket challenger is firmly in calculations.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Backed into 9-1 for recent debut at York (5f, good), where she was slowly away and kept on into sixth of nine; she's with a top yard and could be sharper for that initial experience; she's one to consider.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Runner-up on debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) in April and she has leading claims on that form; however, well beaten on both starts since (5f, AW/good) and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 0-4 and a beaten favourite on her last two starts, but she's run well to be placed on her last three outings and was outstayed when upped to 6f at Carlisle last Monday; the return to 5f could suit and she sets a fair standard for the rest to aim at.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: £6,000 yearling, £10,000 2yo breeze-ups; first foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including Listed; RPR 103), half-sister to winners Fastar (useful 6f-1m) and Sneem (US 5f); this Zoustar newcomer may be best watched.

RaceName: GTC 10th Anniversary EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to three winners but always behind on debut at Ayr (6f, soft) last Saturday; she's probably one for further down the line.

Five places and about 2l ahead of Mini Mac at Doncaster latest (1m, good), his best run for current yard; one win off 12lb higher and a 1l second (both mid-80s RPRs) from two previous visits to Leicester, and while both of those came over 1m he's still effective at 7f; visored last time but not today.

Triple 6f scorer on Tapeta or Polytrack, and ought to have races in him on turf also judged on last month's Chepstow third (good to soft); this trip still seemed too far for now at Redcar latest, however.

Not yet a year since he almost defied 8lb higher on quick turf (1m); mixed bag since, and hard to find any benefit from a third wind operation in the winter, but he's too well treated not to bear in mind at least.

Yarmouth and Salisbury 6f winner in the spring (good and good to firm) off at least 7lb lower, but diminishing returns in the cheekpieces since and visor is now reached for; she's still that bit more compelling at that sprint trip, regardless of posting one of her better 7f efforts latest.
Better on turf than a 1-21 record might suggest; two recent backward steps (never travelling here latest) but recorded a pair of near misses here before those, including over C&D off 2lb higher.

A repeat of the form which netted him a C&D second last month (good to firm) off today's mark would take him close; hard to blame his Doncaster flop since on the return to a 1m trip he has won over before.
Close fifth at Thirsk (5f, good) this month before winning at Southwell (5f, AW) last Thursday; strong claims under a 5lb penalty.

Three-time turf winner (one over C&D) who is 5lb lower than when winning at Newcastle (5f, AW) last December; however, he's struggled since April and a major turnaround is needed in first-time cheekpieces.

9yo who has a regressive profile and was last of six over 6f here a fortnight ago; however, six days earlier he showed spark when third here (6f) and it's possible the return to 5f will help this three-time course winner.

Front-runner who won over C&D last August and at Pontefract (5f, good) on June 8 this year; 4l fourth of six at Nottingham (5f, soft) three days later, which may have come too soon; could make a bold bid.
Won at Newcastle (6f, AW) in April and she's finished close up in two of her three starts since (5f, good/AW); not the most consistent but she can be bang there if she's on another going day.
Inconsistent and well beaten here (6f, good) last Wednesday but he's popped up with a few good runs this year, including when winning here (6f, good to soft) this month; not written off.

Still capable of winning off 30lb higher exactly two years ago, but basically consistent at a lower level these days, and still well suited by a galloping turf track; this step up to 1m4f looks long overdue.

1m2f winner here; back on track with this month's Bath third (1m3f, good) off 1lb higher; needs to back that up, having not been finishing his races off as well for a while previously, including behind Myna back here two starts ago.

Immediate uplift for the switch to 1m2f classifieds, going close at Lingfield before an easy win at Ffos Las two days ago (good); likely stayer of further than that in this sphere as a former bumper winner, and mark looks no worse than fair; chance if this race doesn't come too soon.

Last month's C&D success (good to firm) took his record in handicaps here to 3241; no surprise were he to reverse previous 1m2f course placings with Myna despite a 4lb re-raise, and Liam Wright can claim more here than in that apprentice race last time; player.
Still early days for her on turf, but well held by Coverbridge and (especially) Clipsham Noble when a weakening 16l sixth over C&D latest (good to firm); 2lb drop doesn't tempt.
0-14 and, apart from one early bid for glory on Polytrack five starts ago, he is mostly struggling to break out of rear this term; opposed.
0-13; kept on at Lingfield latest (good to firm) without improving for the step up to 1m2f, and he has further still to travel this time.
14-race losing run now stretches back two years, with no trip this year (tried between 7f and 1m6f, including C&D) provoking a revival.

He's failed to shine on both starts this season but is on a handy mark on last season's best form and is likely to get back on track sooner or later; today's drop back in grade could help in that regard.

Ran just twice last year and soundly beaten each time but he's well handicapped on his 2024 form and he reappeared last month with a close third at Thirsk (5f, good to firm); strong claims if backing that up.

Short-headed on stable/seasonal debut at Musselburgh (5f, soft) in April; excuses at Newmarket (7f, good) next time and he returned to form when fourth of 14 at Thirsk (6f, good); one to consider back down in grade.

Runner-up on two of his starts this spring (both 5f, good) and he was contesting the competitive Dash at Epsom (5f, good to soft) when mid-division last time; this isn't so hot and he could be in the mix.

Running well in defeat this season prior to narrow win in a big field at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; caught wide at Chester (5f, good to soft) 12 days ago when a respectable sixth of 12; could have a big say.

Made all over C&D (5f, good) at the start of the month on his reappearance and runner-up at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) and Ayr (6f, soft) on next two starts; has a solid record here and he holds solid claims.

Bounced back from below-par run at York with close fourth at Pontefract (5f, good) on Sunday, and his best run this season came when close second of 13 over this C&D (good) last month; not ruled out back here.

Beaten just a nose at Newcastle (5f, AW) in March and close fifth of 13 over this C&D (good) last month; might not be far away if he's on a going day but he's been up and down this season and was soundly beaten at Thirsk (5f, good) last time.

Four wins last May/June, the first of them over C&D (good to firm), and she got back on the scoresheet at Musselburgh (5f, good) in April; denied a clear run when beaten just a neck at Musselburgh (5f, good) three weeks ago and it will be no surprise if she makes amends this evening.
Made low-key seasonal/stable debut over 6f last month but placed twice over 5f since, more recently when second on AW a week ago; back in action before 2lb rise takes effect; respected.

Scored off today's mark over 5f on Tapeta in November and is also suited by fast turf but returned from four-month break with disappointing 6f run 19 days ago and needs to have taken significant benefit from that outing.

Has a mixed record this season and is not the force of old but ran a big race from the front when clear second to a dual subsequent winner at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) last month and will be fine on the faster ground here; could be hard to catch if in similar form again.
Out of form this season (four runs) and remains without a win since summer 2024; can't be recommended from out of the handicap.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Grade 2 bumper winner in 2023; useful hurdler subsequently; he's run okay recently over about 1m3f on his first two Flat starts but will be of greater interest when tackling further in handicaps.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000euros yearling, 6,000gns 2yo; eighth foal; brother to French 1m2f/1m4f winner Shahnameh, half-brother to French 1m4f winner Shahinda; dam 1m4f-1m7f winner (including Listed); probably best watched on debut.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Built upon his debut sixth at Beverley (1m2f, good) when keeping on for fourth of ten at Redcar (1m, good) last month; today's extra yardage is likely to be in his favour and he's an each-way contender.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He's improved with each of his three starts, finishing 1l fourth at Southwell (1m, AW) last month and second at Windsor (1m, good to soft) most recently; leading claims for top Newmarket trainer who won this in 2023 and 2024.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Considerable promise when placed at York last autumn on his two 2yo starts (7f/1m, good); disappointing on reappearance at Doncaster in March (1m2f, good to soft; hung badly right) but gelded since and wears first-time blinkers; key player if rediscovering last season's promise.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: First foal; dam French 6.5f AW winner (RPR 73), sister to 6f Group 1 winner Harry Angel, half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Pierre Lapin; takes on rivals who have already shown useful form but she's one to watch in the betting on debut.

RaceName: Indigo Unified Comms Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Down the field on debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in May and tailed off at 66-1 at Pontefract (1m2f, good) since; transformation needed.

Slowly away and mostly in rear on all three 7f novice/maiden starts; more positive signs late on at Catterick last time, however, and the seeming need for longer trip such as this is partly backed up by pedigree.
Neck ahead of Fille Imbassee on identical terms when third over C&D last month (good to firm); appeal undiminished by Ffos Las blowout since, when not taking to soft ground; 2lb badly in.

Still finding her level on turf, but only a neck behind Zooter when fourth in a C&D 0-70 latest (good to firm), the new trip no issue; bit more needed despite slight ease in grade; trainer took this last year.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Onsite Hotel Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last month before respectable sixth at Thirsk (1m, good) and close second here (8.3f, good); comfortably held in fourth over 1m at Carlisle yesterday; takes a sizeable step up in trip but her trainer's horses are frequently versatile in terms of distance; might be a player if she stays.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Onsite Hotel Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Not at the top of her game this season but she's dropped down the weights and took a step back in the right direction when fifth of seven here (1m5f, good) three weeks ago; the drop back in trip could help; not ruled out.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Onsite Hotel Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on for promising fourth in 1m2f novice at Sandown (good) on reappearance then beaten just over 4l when sixth of nine on handicap debut at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm); that latest form has worked out extremely well and this unexposed 3yo can play a leading role.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Onsite Hotel Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on for encouraging third on debut at Leicester (1m, good to soft) last October, two lesser runs have followed, and she was well beaten in a C&D maiden on her recent reappearance, but this handicap newcomer is not written off; trainer won this with a 3yo last year.

RaceName: Dr T B Connors Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Much improved gelding who won recent handicaps at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) and Brighton (1m, good to firm) with quite a bit left in the tank; has a 12lb penalty to shoulder but still commands respect.

RaceName: Dr T B Connors Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Made low-key seasonal/handicap debut when midfield at Thirsk (6f, good) three weeks ago and is now 0-5 as a sprinter; might have needed that recent outing and this new trip could plausibly unlock improvement.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Did well on AW for new stable in March/April, winning three times over 6f, and was very close second off today's mark when back on turf here (5f, good) in early May; needs to up his game after three lesser efforts but still enters calculations.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Regressive 4yo who has dropped 15lb since the autumn; kept on pretty well from the rear for third of seven at Ripon (6f, good) last week, though, and this stiffer track ought to suit him better.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Belatedly opened account at Newcastle (5f, AW) in February and has largely remained in good form since, albeit he was a shade disappointing when only sixth of ten at Ripon (6f, good; favourite) three weeks ago; can make his presence felt.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Battled well for narrow C&D (good to soft) win a fortnight ago and lost out only by a neck over C&D (good) last week; back in action before latest 1lb nudge takes effect; likely contender again.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Largely consistent mare who was respectable third of seven when dropped back in trip for C&D (good to soft) amateur riders' race a fortnight ago; probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo filly who belied 200-1 odds when third in Ayr maiden (6f, good to firm) last month and arguably ran better than finishing position suggests when fading sixth on recent handicap debut at Carlisle (6f, good); others have more compelling claims, though.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Construction Race Day Nights Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Yet to be placed after six starts this season (5f-7f) but won off a much higher mark last spring, at Ripon (6f, good to soft), and may yet turn things around; worth a glance in the betting.
